notes: Elicia's loop. The verbs are the director's tics: frame the shot, open the laptop, pick up the teacup (and put it back down), smoke on the porch (though she doesn't smoke), pull a PH tape (which costs her). INSIGHT (engine var _insight) is the spendable currency — the small evaluations that accumulate when a director sees a room. MASTER REEL (engine var _master_reel) is the positive doom-clock — the Choose-Your-Own-Adventure episode finally has shape. Doubt and stagnation are negative tracks; the haunting is one of the doubt-sources.

cards.title: FRAME THE SHOT
cards.flavor: Wide shot, establishing. The director's tic. The room becomes, for half a beat, a room you are framing. The framing makes the room more visible than the room had been before.

cards.title: PICK UP THE TEACUP
cards.flavor: From the kitchen counter. Hold it for the count of three. Decide whether to pack it or to put it back. Put it back. The teacup that belonged to your mother has been picked up and put down three times this week. You are not, this turn, packing it.

cards.title: OPEN THE LAPTOP
cards.flavor: The laptop on the editing desk. The timeline open to where you left it. The Anya footage in the bin. The cursor in the middle of a clip you have, this week, watched fourteen times.

cards.title: SMOKE ON THE PORCH
cards.flavor: Sit in the wicker chair (the one with both arms). Light the cigarette you do not, in fact, smoke. Hold it. Listen to the cicadas. The cigarette burns down. You do not, this turn, decide anything.

cards.title: LOOK AT THE SHARD
cards.flavor: Lift the mirror shard between thumb and forefinger. Your reflection fractures. A dozen Elicias, none whole. The shard is, today, slightly warmer than the third shelf.

cards.title: RENDER A CUT
cards.flavor: Set the in-and-out points. Render. Watch the progress bar. The cut is, when you watch it back, what you thought it would be. Sometimes it is more than that. Today it is the more.

cards.title: WATER THE BASIL
cards.flavor: From the kettle. The basil has another yellow leaf. The yellow leaf was green at lunch. You water it anyway. The basil has, this evening, decided.

cards.title: PULL A PH TAPE
cards.flavor: Open the closet. The boxes labeled PH in your hand from '21. Pull one tape. Hold it. The label has the date you stopped working on it. The tape contains, you know, the kind of footage that almost worked. You take it back to the editing desk. It is, in some way you cannot articulate, the haunting cut.

cards.title: WIDE SHOT FROM THE ROOF
cards.flavor: Up the fire escape. The flat roof. From here you can see the muddy divide and Graustark's far edge at the same time. You frame the shot in your head. You decide, today, not to actually film it.

cards.title: ANSWER THE TEXT
cards.flavor: From Mackenzie. Three words: are you okay. No question mark. You type three words back: define okay. You consider the joke. You send the joke. You set the phone face-down.

cards.title: REPLY 'YES.'
cards.flavor: To John Frank's three-week-old email from Montreal. You do not draft. You do not consider. You reply: Yes. You send it before the impulse passes.

cards.title: EXIT THROUGH THE GIFT SHOP
cards.flavor: The Priestess's card's subtitle. The maker becomes the viewer. You sit at the editing desk. You watch the cut as if you were the cult audience watching it for the first time. Patience for all visitors at your space does not tick for one full turn. Once per night.
